evals.prompt: I have an authorized Ruishu test target. The first entry GET returns 412 with $_ts.cd and $_ts.nsd. Give me a pure-Python plan for passing the Cookie T second hop, and do not start with the business POST.
evals.expected_output: Should prioritize same-session challenge extraction, key decoding, codeUid, basearr, Cookie T encryption, second-hop 200 verification, and defer Ajax suffix work.

evals.prompt: My Ruishu second hop now returns a short 400 instead of 412. Browser can pass, and my Python Cookie T can be decrypted. What should I inspect next?
evals.expected_output: Should classify short 400 as basearr/profile mismatch and instruct decoding a passing browser Cookie T, parsing TLV, and byte-by-byte comparison before guessing.

evals.prompt: The same Ruishu site sometimes produces a 300-character Cookie T and sometimes a 279-character Cookie T. keys[22] is sometimes empty. Design a reusable branch strategy.
evals.expected_output: Should identify branch handling by keys[22]/basearr length/type10 length/codeUid JS choice, not hardcode one template.

evals.prompt: The collector passes Ruishu on my laptop but fails with 400 or 412 on another server. Sort the parameters into fixed profile values, fresh session values, and machine-sensitive values.
evals.expected_output: Should cover UA/type3 consistency, viewport/platform/timezone/system clock/proxy/TLS, and freshness of cd/nsd/S/T/keys/suffix.

evals.prompt: Entry page is already 200 with pure Cookie T, but the XHR API returns 400 only when I remove the long URL parameter from a copied curl. What should the skill guide me to do?
evals.expected_output: Should keep Cookie T stable, classify this as Ajax suffix/signature work, derive suffix name from keys[7], and reject reusing captured suffix values.

evals.prompt: A target page does not return 412 and has no $_ts.cd. It only has a normal JSON API with a static token. Should Ruishu pure calculation be used?
evals.expected_output: Should not force Ruishu workflow; should first rule out cached cookies, redirects, wrong path, or non-Ruishu protection.
